title: quadlet: Add device support for .volume files
body:
The Device, Type, Copy and Options keys are now supported in quadlet .volume files. This allows users to create filesystem based volumes with quadlets .volume files.

Closes #16896

Signed-off-by: Ingo Becker <ingo@orgizm.net>

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
The `Device`, `Type`, `Copy` and `Options` keys are now supported in quadlet .volume files.
```

comments.body:
I implemented the two additional keys `Type` and `Options` that i didn't mention in the issue. It just makes sense to implement all the options related to filesystems. Concerning the `Options` key, there is an overlap with the already implemented `User` and `Group` keys. The changes are fully compatible with them but also allow users who aren't aware of the `User` and `Group` key to set those options using the `Options` key instead. I'm unsure if some validation regarding those options is needed if they are supplied to the `Options` key instead of the more appropriate `Group` and `User` keys.

The `Type` and `Options` keys are silently ignored if no `Device` option is supplied because they make no sense without it. I'm not sure if it might be better to raise an error in this case.
I think you should raise an error, or at least a warning, so users who think the flags are doing something realize their misunderstanding.

This is a bit of a slippery slope, but why didn't you implement the "image" option and the "copy" option.
The "image" option would be kind of nice, for mounting an image into a container.
I think we might need to also add subpath to quadlet, once https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16913 merges.

> This is a bit of a slippery slope, but why didn't you implement the "image" option and the "copy" option. The "image" option would be kind of nice, for mounting an image into a container.

I agree for the `copy` option. But i'm not entirely sure how to implement the `image` option. I could introduce a key `Image` which raises an error for all other options which are not valid for the `image` driver. Without explicitly specifying a `Driver` in the .volume file, there is kind of an implicit distinction taking place for drivers by choosing a valid combination of keys. This blurs the fact that there is a concept of different `drivers`. And how to deal with plugins then?
I added the `Copy` key and raised an error for the `Type`/`Options` without `Device` case.

I think we can leave other errors to the podman command that this will execute.  No reason to make quadlet parse antyhing but the simplest objects, especially where things could change over time.  We might eventually allow one of these other options with a "image" volume for example.  Leave the additional parsing to Podman command.
@ingobecker Are you still working on this?

@ingobecker: PR needs rebase.
